headline: SLOP WATCH ISSUED OVER THE FACEBOOK DELTA AS SUNDAY DEVOTIONAL-BAIT SYSTEM PARKS OFFSHORE, HARVESTING BLESSINGS AT SCALE
advisory: A slow-moving low-pressure system of synthetic devotional imagery has stalled over the Facebook Delta and is expected to linger through the morning service hours. Conditions favor impossible architecture, weeping figures rendered with too many fingers, and captions instructing residents to type a single word for a guaranteed blessing. The Service stresses that the faith is not the hazard; the harvest is. Engagement collected during this event is monetized downstream and does not, per available instrumentation, reach any higher authority.

regional_outlook.conditions: Saturated. Heavy devotional bait through midday, with scattered cathedrals assembled from architecture that disobeys gravity. Comment sections rising rapidly, mostly single-word amens.
Murky, with a craft-tutorial haze drifting in. Inspirational board pins lead nowhere on tap; residents advised not to wade in expecting instructions.
Patchy digital-download fog. Several 'printable prayer kits' detected; files reportedly open to a single blurred page.
Calm but eerie. Ambient 'worship for focus' playlists generated overnight by no known musician. Pleasant, weightless, attributable to nobody.
Dry with rising clutter. A bloom of identical meditation apps sharing one stock sunrise and four reviews each.

specimen.status: Abundant; weekly peak observed at dawn on the Sabbath, dispersing by Monday rush.
specimen.habitat: Sprawling feeds of the Facebook Delta and adjacent shallows; favors pages with reverent names and no contact information.
specimen.field_marks: Improbably ornate scene — angels with surplus wings, candles that cast no light, faces softened to wax. Always paired with a caption that gently dares you not to engage.
specimen.call: 99% will scroll past. Type AMEN in 5 seconds and watch what happens.
specimen.confusion_species: A sincere post from an actual congregation or community member. Tell them apart by the demand: a real church shares an event, a time, a place; the Trap demands a one-word reflex and offers a vague cosmic payout with a hard countdown.

spotter_tip: If a post promises a blessing in exchange for typing a single word within a strict deadline, that deadline is for the algorithm, not for you. The reward never arrives because there is no one at the other end to send it.
tomorrow: Devotional system breaks up by Monday morning, displaced by a returning Motivation front building over the LinkedIn Plateau.
